summary:
A shorter sync where Adam shared more Alpha Claw usage including building a 'Superstar' research app for a friend's gala event (found deep YouTube videos and old photos that even the subject had forgotten about), and setting up Supabase for persistent data storage. Brad shared that he set up monitoring for Alpha Claw at 50 cents/month and built an automated Google Ads manager while riding in a car in LA that creates ads, manages budgets, and optimizes based on statistically significant results.

They discussed Cameron finding a bug where OpenClaw was spawning multiple instances of itself, jamming up ports. Brad mentioned security updates including Docker-in-Docker sandboxing were deployed and wanted to ensure Adam could still build apps. They briefly discussed needing clear pricing/FAQ communication on the Alpha Agent website regarding API costs (e.g., $200/month for heavy usage could be a blocker for individual users like Diana). Brad had to jump to a demo call.
keyDiscussionPoints: Superstar research app demo - deep web research on individuals
Supabase integration set up for persistent data
Alpha Claw monitoring set up at $0.50/month
Automated Google Ads manager built by Brad
OpenClaw spawning multiple instances bug found by Cameron
Pricing/FAQ clarity needed on Alpha Agent website for API costs
Alpha Claw as 'new dopamine fix' - highly engaging tool
decisions: Need FAQ/pricing page on Alpha Agent website to clarify API costs
Brad heading to LA, off Monday (Presidents Day)

actionItems.action: Investigate and fix OpenClaw port-jamming/multi-instance spawning bug
Add pricing FAQ to Alpha Agent website
Prepare Alpha Claw demo for IAS Tuesday call
